description: Complete technical specification for the HoliLabs Communications Hub — unified WhatsApp/SMS/Email inbox with LGPD compliance.

bspRecommendation.primary: Direct Meta WhatsApp Cloud API via Twilio
bspRecommendation.reasoning: Twilio provides a single SDK for WhatsApp + SMS + Email. Avoids managing a separate BSP. Meta Cloud API is the standard since On-Premises API EOL October 2025.

takeBlip.pros: Brazilian market leader, PT-BR support team, LGPD-native
takeBlip.cons: Separate SDK from SMS, higher cost at scale

zenvia.pros: Brazilian, multi-channel (WhatsApp + SMS + RCS), good pricing

directMeta.pros: Lowest cost, direct access, no middleman
directMeta.cons: No SMS/Email, must manage webhook infrastructure yourself

steps: 1. Create Twilio account at twilio.com (or use existing)
2. Enable Messaging Service in Twilio Console
3. Register for WhatsApp Business API via Twilio → 'Senders' → 'WhatsApp senders'
4. Submit business verification to Meta (requires: business name, website, address, phone number)
5. Provision a WhatsApp-enabled phone number (Brazilian +55 number recommended for LATAM)
8. Submit HSM message templates for pre-approval (from comms-hub-templates.json)
9. Test in Twilio sandbox before going live

phoneNumberProvisioning.brazil: Brazilian +55 numbers available via Twilio. Requires local address verification. ~$1/month for WhatsApp-enabled number.
phoneNumberProvisioning.recommendation: One number per country. Use the number as the clinic's official WhatsApp.

messageTypes.description: Plain text message up to 4096 characters
Pre-approved HSM template with variable substitution
messageTypes.useCase: Initiating conversation outside 24h window (appointment reminders, lab results)
messageTypes.approval: Submit via Twilio Console → Content Template Builder. Review takes 24-48h.

messageTypes.description: Image, document, audio, or video attachment
messageTypes.useCase: Sharing appointment confirmations, medication guides (NEVER share lab results or diagnoses via media)
messageTypes.ruthInvariant: Media attachments MUST NOT contain PHI. Use secure portal links instead.

messageTypes.description: List of options patient can select

utility.description: Appointment reminders, lab results, prescription updates

service.description: Free within 24h after patient initiates contact

clinic_500_patients.note: Essentially free — massive competitive advantage vs Agenda Pro

twentyFourHourWindow.rule: After a patient sends a message, you have 24h to respond with free-form text. After 24h, only pre-approved templates can initiate conversation.
twentyFourHourWindow.implementation: Track lastInboundAt per conversation. If now - lastInboundAt > 24h, force template selection in compose bar. Show countdown timer in UI.

sms.setup: Use the same TWILIO_SMS_NUMBER configured above

brazilNumbers.recommendation: Start with long code. Migrate to short code only if volume justifies cost.

ucs2.charset: Unicode (needed for accents: ã, ç, é, ñ)

characterLimits.note: Portuguese and Spanish texts typically require UCS-2 due to accented characters. Effective limit: 67 chars per segment for multi-part SMS.

sms.deliveryReceipts: Same webhook as WhatsApp: /api/comms/webhook/status. StatusCallback parameter on send.

email.recommendation: Use Resend (already in .env) for transactional emails. Consider SendGrid only if email volume >50K/month.

transactionalTemplates.rendering: React Email templates (already used for Finalize encounter email)
transactionalTemplates.tracking: Open tracking via Resend. Click tracking for portal links.

unsubscribe.requirement: LGPD Art. 7 §3: patient must be able to opt-out of any email category at any time
unsubscribe.implementation: List-Unsubscribe header on every email. One-click unsubscribe link in footer. Granular preferences in patient portal consent page.

externalId.description: WhatsApp conversation ID or Twilio ConversationSid

assignedToId.description: Staff member assigned to this conversation

content.description: Encrypted at rest via encryptPHIWithVersion. CYRUS invariant.

mediaUrl.description: URL for media attachments (stored in secure bucket, not Twilio CDN)

templateId.description: HSM template ID if sent via template

twilioSid.description: Twilio MessageSid for idempotency and status tracking

metadata.description: Channel-specific data (WhatsApp profile pic URL, etc.)

existingModelsToCheck.note: holilabsv2 already has Conversation, ConversationParticipant, ConversationMessage models. Verify they match this spec or migrate. Key additions needed: channelType, externalId, lastInboundAt, twilioSid, status enum, contentType enum.

realtime.reasoning: SSE is simpler than WebSocket, works through proxies, auto-reconnects. Sufficient for message delivery updates (not real-time chat which needs <100ms).

events.trigger: Twilio incoming webhook receives a message

clientImplementation.reconnect: Automatic reconnect with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, max 30s)
clientImplementation.heartbeat: Server sends `event: ping` every 30s to keep connection alive

send: 1. User clicks Send → message immediately appears in UI with status 'Sending...' (optimistic)
2. Message added to local IndexedDB queue
3. If online: POST /api/comms/send → on success, update status to 'Sent' → on failure, show retry button
4. If offline: keep in queue, show offline indicator
5. On reconnect: flush queue in order, update statuses
receive: 1. SSE connection drops → show 'Slow connection' or 'Offline' indicator
2. On reconnect: SSE resumes, client fetches missed messages via GET /api/comms/conversations/:id?since=lastMessageTimestamp
3. Merge server messages with local state, deduplicate by twilioSid

search.approach: PostgreSQL pg_trgm + GIN index for full-text message search
search.reasoning: Meilisearch is already in the stack for patient search. For messages, pg_trgm is simpler (no separate service to manage) and sufficient for clinic-scale message volumes (<100K messages).

implementation.migration: CREATE INDEX idx_message_content_trgm ON Message USING GIN (content gin_trgm_ops);
implementation.query: SELECT * FROM Message WHERE content ILIKE '%search_term%' AND conversation_id IN (SELECT id FROM Conversation WHERE organization_id = $orgId) ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 20;
implementation.alternative: If message volume exceeds 1M, migrate to Meilisearch index (already configured in the app).

cyrusInvariants.implementation: import twilio from 'twilio'; const isValid = twilio.validateRequest(process.env.TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN, req.headers['x-twilio-signature'], fullUrl, req.body); if (!isValid) return res.status(403).json({ error: 'Invalid signature' });
cyrusInvariants.vetoLevel: SUPREME — without this, anyone can spoof patient messages

cyrusInvariants.implementation: Use encryptPHIWithVersion on Message.content field. Decrypt only when rendering in authenticated UI.
Every conversation query includes WHERE organizationId = session.organizationId. Never expose conversation IDs in URLs without re-checking ownership.
Every message send creates AuditLog entry with action=MESSAGE_SENT, entityType=Message, entityId=message.id, accessReason=TREATMENT
Deduplicate incoming messages by twilioSid. If Message with twilioSid already exists, return 200 without processing (replay protection).

ruthInvariants.implementation: Before any WhatsApp send: check patient.whatsappConsentGiven === true AND patient.whatsappConsentWithdrawnAt === null. For SMS: check patient.smsEnabled. For Email: check patient.emailEnabled.
ruthInvariants.optIn: Explicit opt-in required. Never assume consent. Portal consent page: /portal/onboarding/consent
ruthInvariants.optOut: Honor within 24h. Set whatsappConsentWithdrawnAt = now(). Stop all future messages immediately.
ruthInvariants.granularity: Consent is per-channel AND per-category (appointment reminders, medication reminders, lab alerts, marketing). Patient can opt-in to appointment reminders but opt-out of marketing.

ruthInvariants.implementation: Content filter scans outbound message for clinical keywords (diagnóstico, resultado, glicemia, pressão, HbA1c, etc.) and blocks if found. Force use of portal links for PHI access.

Configurable per organization: 90 days (default), 180 days, 365 days, permanent. Expired messages: soft-delete content (replace with '[Message expired per retention policy]'), keep metadata + AuditLog.
ruthInvariants.lgpdArticle: LGPD Art. 15 — data must be deleted when purpose is fulfilled or retention period expires

ruthInvariants.implementation: Patient requests deletion → soft-delete all Message.content in their conversations → replace with '[Deleted per patient request]' → preserve AuditLog entries (LGPD Art. 37 requires audit retention even after data erasure)
ruthInvariants.process: Patient request → ORG_ADMIN approval → batch soft-delete → audit entry with action=DATA_ERASURE_REQUEST

ruthInvariants.implementation: All HSM templates reviewed for SaMD-safe language before submission to Meta. No templates contain: 'diagnose', 'detect', 'treat', 'cure', 'predict disease'. Templates stored in comms-hub-templates.json — use ONLY approved templates.

layout.description: Two-panel layout (ConversationList + MessageThread) with optional PatientContextSidebar

patientContext.trigger: Click patient name in thread header

rendering.timestamp: Relative (1m, 2h, 3d) for <7 days, absolute date for older
rendering.unreadBadge: Red dot with count if unreadCount > 0

rendering.status: Below outbound messages: single check (sent), double check (delivered), blue double check (read), X (failed + retry button)
rendering.media: Inline image preview (max 300px), document icon + filename for PDFs

rendering.24hWarning: If lastInboundAt > 22h ago: show amber banner 'Service window closing in Xh. After that, only templates can be sent.'
rendering.offlineBanner: If offline: show red banner 'You are offline. Messages will be sent when connection is restored.'

rendering.search: Search by template name or category
rendering.categories: Tabs: All, Appointment, Lab Results, Billing, Prevention, Medication
rendering.preview: Selected template shows body with {{placeholders}} highlighted
rendering.autoFill: Known variables auto-filled from patient context (patient_name, appointment_date, etc.)

sections: Patient header: name, age, sex, photo
Contact: phone, email, WhatsApp consent status
Recent labs: last 3 results with values + reference ranges

badge.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Message WHERE direction = 'INBOUND' AND status != 'READ' AND conversation_id IN (SELECT id FROM Conversation WHERE organization_id = $orgId)
badge.display: Red badge with count. If >99, show '99+'.
badge.polling: SSE event 'conversation_updated' triggers re-query. No polling needed.

migrationChecklist: 1. Add channelType, externalId, lastInboundAt fields to existing Conversation model (or create new if separate)
2. Add twilioSid, status enum, contentType, mediaUrl, errorCode fields to Message model
3. Create ConversationStatus, MessageDirection, ChannelType, MessageContentType, MessageStatus enums if not exist
5. Create /api/comms/ route directory with: send, conversations, webhook/twilio, webhook/status, templates
6. Add Twilio SDK: pnpm add twilio
8. Submit HSM templates via Twilio Console
10. Update sidebar nav label and route
11. Add SSE endpoint for real-time updates
12. Add pg_trgm extension and index for message search
